varieties.name: Agave tequilana Weber Azul (Blue Weber Agave)
varieties.traits: 8-12 years to maturity, blue-gray leaves, rosette 1.5-2m tall
varieties.yield_notes: High sugar content for tequila production; produces aguamiel (sap)
varieties.availability: Australian Agave Project (Whitsundays, QLD), specialty succulent nurseries in humid subtropical climates area

varieties.name: Agave tequilana 'Tequila Sunrise'
varieties.traits: Pale yellow marginal variegation on blue-gray leaves, vigorous grower
varieties.yield_notes: Produces offsets prolifically
varieties.availability: Specialty agave nurseries in Queensland, The Jungle Collective (online delivery to humid subtropical climates)

varieties.name: Agave americana (Century Plant)
varieties.origin: Eastern Mexico; introduced to Europe mid-1500s
varieties.traits: Large rosette 1.8m wide, gray-green leaves with sharp spines, flowers after 10-30 years
varieties.yield_notes: Pale yellow flowers on 5-12m tall branched stalk; produces numerous offsets
varieties.availability: Common at humid subtropical climates nurseries, Hello Hello Plants & Garden Supplies

varieties.name: Agave americana 'Marginata' (Variegated Century Plant)
varieties.origin: Cultivated form of A. americana, widely grown since 1800s
varieties.traits: Twisted silvery-green leaves with bright yellow marginal stripes, 6ft tall x 10ft wide
varieties.yield_notes: Produces well-rooted suckers at base; flowers on 20-30ft tall stalk
varieties.availability: humid subtropical climates nurseries, The Jungle Collective, Hello Hello Plants

varieties.name: Agave americana 'Mediopicta' (White-striped Century Plant)
varieties.traits: White or cream central stripe down leaf center, more compact than 'Marginata'
varieties.yield_notes: Suckers more closely to parent plant than 'Marginata'
varieties.availability: Specialty succulent nurseries Queensland

varieties.name: Agave americana 'Cornelius'
varieties.traits: Marginal variegation with monstrose growth, compact, slow to sucker
varieties.yield_notes: Rarely produces offsets - much safer for landscape use
varieties.availability: Specialty agave collectors, online nurseries

varieties.name: Agave attenuata (Foxtail Agave, Swan's Neck)
varieties.origin: Central Mexico; uncommon in natural habitat
varieties.traits: Spineless! Soft greenish-blue leaves, curved flower spike (4m high), 10+ years to bloom
varieties.yield_notes: Yellowish drooping flowers on arching stalk; offsets develop after flowering
varieties.availability: Common at humid subtropical climates nurseries, Burke's Backyard recommendations, Hello Hello Plants, The Jungle Collective

varieties.name: Agave angustifolia (Narrow-leaved Century Plant, Caribbean Agave)
varieties.origin: Mexico to Central America; naturalized Caribbean and Indian subcontinent since 1800s
varieties.traits: Narrow leaves, rosette to 1m across, thrives in heat with minimal water
varieties.yield_notes: Green to yellow flowers on 5m tall stem; produces numerous offsets
varieties.availability: Common in humid subtropical climates, nurseries throughout Queensland

varieties.name: Agave angustifolia 'Marginata' (Variegated Caribbean Agave)
varieties.traits: White stripes along leaf margins, 4ft tall and wide
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely vigorous offsetting
varieties.availability: Very common in humid subtropical climates area

varieties.name: Agave angustifolia var. espadín (Espadín Agave)
varieties.origin: Oaxaca and throughout Mexico; genetic grandfather of blue agave
varieties.traits: Most common mezcal agave (90% of production), 6-8 years to maturity
varieties.yield_notes: High inulin content for efficient alcohol production; versatile flavor terroir

varieties.name: Agave parryi (Artichoke Agave, Parry's Agave)
varieties.origin: Arizona, New Mexico to northern Mexico; high elevations
varieties.traits: Striking artichoke-like rosette, sharp black spears on bluish-gray leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Tall flower spike with yellow to reddish flowers
varieties.availability: Specialty succulent nurseries, Australian Desert Blooms (Ipswich)

varieties.name: Agave parryi var. truncata (Artichoke Agave)
varieties.traits: Striking blue-gray rosettes, clumping habit, 2-3ft tall x 3-4ft wide
varieties.yield_notes: Forms attractive clumps
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, collectors

varieties.name: Agave victoriae-reginae (Queen Victoria Agave)
varieties.origin: Coahuila, Nuevo Leon, Durango, Mexico; 1875 discovery
varieties.traits: Compact (under 2ft), dark green leaves with striking white painted markings, 25-40 years to bloom
varieties.yield_notes: Very slow growing; perfect for containers
varieties.availability: Premium at Australian Desert Blooms, specialty collectors

varieties.name: Agave victoriae-reginae 'Compacta'
varieties.traits: Even smaller and more compact than species, under 12 inches
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely slow growing
varieties.availability: Rare, specialty collectors

varieties.name: Agave ovatifolia (Whale's Tongue Agave)
varieties.traits: Large rosette with broad, oval-shaped powder-blue leaves, 3-5ft tall and wide
varieties.yield_notes: Solitary (doesn't offset), flowers 3-5 years after planting
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, Plant Delights online

varieties.name: Agave desmettiana 'Variegata' (Variegated Dwarf Smooth Agave)
varieties.traits: Elegant urn-shaped form, blue-green leaves with yellow edges, mostly spineless
varieties.yield_notes: Produces many offsets early, forms attractive clumps
varieties.availability: humid subtropical climates area nurseries, Plants Express

varieties.name: Agave geminiflora (Twin Flower Agave, Pincushion Agave)
varieties.origin: Nayarit, Mexico; oak woodlands 3000-7000 feet elevation
varieties.traits: Hundreds of narrow leaves forming dense rounded rosette, 10-15 years to bloom
varieties.yield_notes: 8-12ft tall unbranched flower spike with greenish-yellow flowers in pairs
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, collectors

varieties.name: Agave vilmoriniana (Octopus Agave)
varieties.traits: Unusual rosette 3-4ft high x 5-6ft wide, soft curving leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Curved flowering stalk
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Agave filifera (Thread Agave)
varieties.traits: Dark green leaves with bronze cast, 3ft wide x 2ft high
varieties.yield_notes: Medium-sized
varieties.availability: Specialty succulent nurseries

varieties.name: Agave filifera 'Compacta'
varieties.traits: Miniature version, stays under 1 foot diameter
varieties.yield_notes: Very small, perfect for containers
varieties.availability: Specialty collectors, rare

varieties.name: Agave parviflora (Smallflower Century Plant, Santa Cruz Striped Agave)
varieties.traits: One of smallest agaves: 4-6 inches tall x 6-8 inches wide
varieties.yield_notes: Smallest flowers of all century plants, 3-7ft flower stalk
varieties.availability: Specialty collectors, miniature agave specialists

varieties.name: Agave bracteosa (Spider Agave, Squid Agave)
varieties.traits: Completely spineless! Soft green recurved leaves, compact
varieties.yield_notes: May bloom early (3-5 years)
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, recommended for humid subtropical climates gardens

varieties.name: Agave stricta (Hedgehog Agave)
varieties.traits: Narrow rosettes, spine-tipped dark green leaves in spherical form
varieties.yield_notes: Dense symmetrical rosette
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, succulent collections

varieties.name: Agave weberi (Weber Agave, Weber's Century Plant)
varieties.origin: San Luis Potosí, Tamaulipas, Mexico; naturalized southern Texas
varieties.traits: Large rosette, tested for saline soil tolerance in Australian trials
varieties.yield_notes: Performed well in Queensland saline soil tests alongside A. parryi
varieties.availability: Research sources, specialty collectors

varieties.name: Agave shawii (Shaw's Agave, Coastal Agave)
varieties.origin: Coastal southern California and northwestern Baja California; endangered in USA
varieties.traits: 2-3ft tall and wide, glossy green leaves with red-gray toothed margins
varieties.yield_notes: Forms colonies via offsets, 12ft flower spike
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, coastal plant specialists

varieties.name: Agave colorata (Mescal Ceniza)
varieties.traits: Gray fleshy leaves, 60-120cm diameter, tiny flexible red spines
varieties.yield_notes: Solitary but sometimes produces pups, white/pale-pink/purple flowers
varieties.availability: Specialty agave nurseries, Cacti.com

varieties.name: Agave 'Blue Glow'
varieties.traits: Blue-gray leaves with distinctive red margins, 2-3ft tall and wide
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate offsetting
varieties.availability: Popular at humid subtropical climates nurseries, specialty growers

varieties.name: Agave sisalana (Sisal, Sisal Hemp)
varieties.traits: 7-10 year lifespan, produces 200-250 usable leaves, 150-200cm tall rosette
varieties.yield_notes: Commercial fiber production (4% of plant weight), 6.4 tonnes/hectare fiber
varieties.availability: Traditional agricultural suppliers India, fiber production areas

varieties.name: Agave cantala (Cantala, Maguey de la India)
varieties.origin: Mexico; introduced to India early 1800s, naturalized throughout tropical Asia
varieties.traits: Short stem 30-60cm, rosette with 150-200cm long leaves, 7-9cm wide, 15-30+ year lifespan
varieties.yield_notes: Up to 8m tall flower stem, fiber yield 3-4.5% of leaf weight
varieties.availability: Traditional throughout tropical monsoon climates, agricultural suppliers, naturalized plants

varieties.name: Agave vera-cruz (Veracruz Agave)
varieties.origin: Veracruz and Oaxaca, Mexico; naturalized in Indian subcontinent, Thailand
varieties.traits: Stout perennial herb 30-45cm tall, woody simple stem
varieties.yield_notes: Used for fiber in Burma (Myanmar) - 'longyi' garment industry
varieties.availability: Naturalized populations India, traditional agricultural sources

varieties.name: Agave angustifolia (Narrow-leaved Century Plant)
varieties.origin: Mexico to Central America; naturalized Indian subcontinent since colonial period
varieties.traits: Dwarf form when grown as ornamental, robust when cultivated for fiber
varieties.yield_notes: Used as ornamental and hedge plant, produces usable fiber
varieties.availability: Common throughout tropical monsoon climates, railway plantings, agricultural areas

varieties.name: Agave americana (Century Plant)
varieties.origin: Eastern Mexico; introduced to India by Portuguese in 15th century
varieties.traits: Large rosette, traditional use for medicine, hedging, soil erosion control
varieties.yield_notes: Used for ropes, cordage, mats; stem edible in small quantities
varieties.availability: Common throughout India including tropical monsoon climates, naturalized populations

varieties.name: Agave vivipara
varieties.origin: Caribbean islands (Aruba, Bonaire, Curaçao); introduced to India, Bangladesh, Nepal, Pakistan
varieties.traits: Produces vegetative bulbils prolifically on flower stalk
varieties.yield_notes: Bulbils provide easy propagation, flowers produce minimal seed
varieties.availability: Traditional Indian sources, naturalized areas

varieties.name: Agave attenuata (Foxtail Agave)
varieties.origin: Central Mexico; widely cultivated tropics and subtropics
varieties.traits: SPINELESS! Soft pliable leaves, curved flower spike, 4-5ft tall and 6-8ft wide
varieties.yield_notes: Popular ornamental worldwide, produces offsets
varieties.availability: Ornamental nurseries throughout India, tropical monsoon climates garden centers

varieties.name: Agave americana 'Marginata' (Variegated Century Plant)
varieties.origin: Cultivated form of A. americana, grown India since colonial times
varieties.traits: Yellow marginal stripes, more ornamental than species
varieties.yield_notes: Produces suckers at base
varieties.availability: Ornamental nurseries tropical monsoon climates, specialty plant centers

varieties.name: Agave tequilana Weber Azul (Blue Weber Agave)
varieties.traits: 8-12 years to maturity, blue-gray ornamental leaves
varieties.yield_notes: High sugar content, ornamental use in tropics
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, may need to source from outside tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Agave angustifolia var. espadín (Espadín Mezcal Agave)
varieties.origin: Oaxaca, Mexico; most common mezcal agave (90% of production)
varieties.traits: 6-8 years to maturity, genetic ancestor of blue agave
varieties.yield_notes: High inulin content, efficient sugar conversion

varieties.name: Agave salmiana (Pulque Agave, Giant Agave)
varieties.origin: Central and southern Mexico; most used species for pulque production
varieties.traits: VERY LARGE - 8-13ft tall and wide, 15-40ft flower stalk, 15-25 years to bloom
varieties.yield_notes: Large sap production for pulque, aguamiel harvesting

varieties.name: Agave mapisaga (Mapisaga Pulque Agave)
varieties.origin: Central Mexico; highest sap production for pulque
varieties.traits: Immense size, highest daily sap yield of all pulque agaves
varieties.yield_notes: Superior aguamiel (sap) production

varieties.name: Agave fourcroydes (Henequen)
varieties.origin: Southern Mexico, Guatemala; major plantation fiber agave of Yucatán
varieties.traits: Rosette 1.2-1.8m wide, sword-shaped leaves 1.2-1.8m long
varieties.yield_notes: Fiber production for rope and twine, sterile hybrid (propagated by suckers/bulbils)
varieties.availability: Would need to source from fiber agave suppliers, Mexico

varieties.name: Agave vilmoriniana (Octopus Agave)
varieties.traits: Unusual rosette 3-4ft x 5-6ft, soft curving leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Architectural form

varieties.name: Agave shawii (Shaw's Agave, Coastal Agave)
varieties.origin: Coastal southern California, Baja California - maritime environments
varieties.traits: 2-3ft tall and wide, adapted to coastal conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Forms colonies
varieties.availability: Would need specialty sourcing, coastal plant nurseries

varieties.name: Agave bracteosa (Spider Agave)
varieties.traits: SPINELESS! Soft green leaves, completely safe
varieties.yield_notes: May bloom early (3-5 years)
varieties.availability: Specialty succulent nurseries, may need online ordering

varieties.name: Agave colorata (Mescal Ceniza)
varieties.traits: Gray-blue fleshy leaves, compact 60-120cm diameter
varieties.yield_notes: Ornamental, mezcal production in native range
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, may need import

varieties.name: Agave desmettiana 'Variegata' (Variegated Dwarf Smooth Agave)
varieties.traits: Urn-shaped, blue-green with yellow edges, mostly spineless
varieties.yield_notes: Early offsetting

varieties.name: Agave striata (Hedgehog Agave)
varieties.traits: Narrow rosettes, spherical form, spine-tipped leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Architectural specimen
varieties.availability: Specialty succulent sources

varieties.name: Agave geminiflora (Twin Flower Agave)
varieties.origin: Nayarit, Mexico; native habitat receives 40 inches rainfall summer-autumn-winter
varieties.traits: Hundreds of narrow leaves, dense rounded rosette, 10-15 years to bloom
varieties.yield_notes: 8-12ft flower spike with twin flowers
varieties.availability: Specialty collectors, rare

varieties.name: Agave parviflora (Smallflower Century Plant)
varieties.traits: Miniature! 4-6 inches tall x 6-8 inches wide
varieties.yield_notes: Smallest agave flowers
varieties.availability: Miniature agave specialists, collectors

varieties.name: Agave 'Blue Glow'
varieties.traits: Blue-gray leaves with red margins, 2-3ft tall and wide
varieties.yield_notes: Forms clumps
varieties.availability: Popular hybrid, ornamental nurseries
